claims.claim: Finite tie-aware trajectory-selection law is implemented and tested.
High N can help aligned diffusion trajectory selection.
High N can hurt or saturate under scorer misalignment.
Low diversity reduces marginal value of N.
N versus K tradeoff is measured.
Latency-adjusted utility can prefer smaller N or K.
Calibrated scorer repairs high-N selection in at least one regime.
Audit-Then-Sample chooses when to increase N, stop early, repair, increase diversity, reduce K, or block high-N selection.

claims.claim: Sequential deployment stress shows zero false admits and exposes the utility cost of conservative high-N blocking.
Project is not a WAM clone.
claims.evidence: docs/differentiation_from_wam_jepa.md centers diffusion action trajectories rather than imagined rollouts.

claims.claim: Project is not a JEPA clone.
claims.evidence: docs/differentiation_from_wam_jepa.md forbids latent-prediction framing as the central contribution.

claims.claim: All major claims are backed by CSV/JSON artifacts.
Learned experiment satisfies the Diffusion Policy-style validity checklist and includes multi-seed state/image ID/OOD evaluation.

claims.claim: PushT reports selected rollout coverage and success trajectory-search curves from actual simulator rollouts.
A second standard Gymnasium Robotics FetchPush benchmark tests the same trajectory-search law under MuJoCo manipulation rollouts.
N versus K recommendations are backed by abstract budget sweeps and measured true-DDPM, PushT, and FetchPush runtime.
Global diffusion-policy wording is promoted only when true-DDPM, PushT, and FetchPush rollout-metric tiers are supported.

artifact_backing.claim: All major claims are backed by CSV/JSON artifacts.

calibration.claim: Calibrated scorer repairs high-N selection in at least one regime.

controlled_sampler.claim: High N can help aligned diffusion trajectory selection.
High N can hurt or saturate under scorer misalignment.

controller_fix.claim: Audit-Then-Sample chooses when to increase N, stop early, repair, increase diversity, reduce K, or block high-N selection.

denoising_budget.claim: N versus K tradeoff is measured.

deployment_stress.claim: Sequential deployment stress shows zero false admits and exposes the utility cost of conservative high-N blocking.

differentiation.claim: Project is not a WAM clone.
differentiation.evidence: docs/differentiation_from_wam_jepa.md centers diffusion action trajectories rather than imagined rollouts.

differentiation.claim: Project is not a JEPA clone.
differentiation.evidence: docs/differentiation_from_wam_jepa.md forbids latent-prediction framing as the central contribution.

diversity.claim: Low diversity reduces marginal value of N.

fetch_robotics_benchmark.claim: A second standard Gymnasium Robotics FetchPush benchmark tests the same trajectory-search law under MuJoCo manipulation rollouts.

finite_law.claim: Finite tie-aware trajectory-selection law is implemented and tested.

latency.claim: Latency-adjusted utility can prefer smaller N or K.
N versus K recommendations are backed by abstract budget sweeps and measured true-DDPM, PushT, and FetchPush runtime.
